name: Crew Cabin
description: The crew cabin, there is a basic double bunk on the far wall next to a %storage locker% and a #small desk#. A door leads to *main corridor CH-1*.

name: Captains Quarters
description: Your personal quarters. The hum from the HVAC vent fills the air. Dim lights flicker on automatically.
The bunk is unmade and your personal effects are scattered, reminders of a life spent in the black.
A #metal desk# is bolted to the floor beside your bunk, on top of it sits your !personal terminal!
A %locker% with twin sliding doors is built into the far wall beside a #long shelf#. A hatch leads to *main corridor CH-1*.

name: Bathroom
description: A compact, utilitarian wet room. The air is humid, carrying the faint tang of recycled water and sanitizer.
There is a sink atop a %small cabinet%, vacuum toilet, and steam shower, all the function with none of the luxury.
The single hatch leads back to *sub corridor CH-2*.

name: Galley
description: A cramped kitchen area. Food rehydrator, turbo oven and water recycler, a #small table# is bolted to the floor.
Everything you need to prepare a bland and unexciting meal.
A hatch leads back to the *recreation room*.

name: Recreation Room
description: ~The curved hull walls close in around an amber leather sofa — too large, too expensive, entirely wrong for a cargo hauler. A stained rug covers part of the deck, the rest scattered with food wrappers, old holovids and the general detritus of a man living alone in deep space.~
^power_state^

The *Cockpit* is through the forward door, the *Main Corridor* through the aft hatch. The *Galley* is through the starboard hatch. Port side doors lead to the *Med-Bay* and *Storage Room*. A #Shelving Unit# runs along the starboard bulkhead, a ?Power Junction? is mounted beside the aft hatch on the port side.
description_powered: Strip lighting hums overhead. The forward screen loops between an Enso VeilTech corporate wellness programme nobody asked for and product infomercials nobody watches — ways to spend what's left of your meagre salary after corporate deductions.
description_unpowered: The strip lighting is dead. Photoluminescent deck markers cast just enough green light to navigate carefully. The forward screen is dark and silent — a small mercy. Something somewhere has gone wrong.

name: Cockpit
description: The command center of the ship. The pilot's console, !navigation terminal!, and sensor displays dominate the space.
Two well worn but quite comfortable synth-leather seats are securely attached to the floor, the viewscreen ahead frames the vastness of space. A solid door leads back to the *recreation room*.

name: Storage Room
description: The ships designated storage room for spare parts and crew consumables.

An automated storage facility is built into the rear wall this can be accessed via the integrated !inventory terminal!. Opposite to this is a #metal bench#. A hatch leads back to the *recreation room*.

name: Hyper-sleep Chamber
description: A cramped room with dual stasis pods and various life support equipment.
A vital system to survive the month long journeys between worlds.
An access door leads back to the *med-bay*.

name: Med-Bay
description: A small medical facility with diagnostic equipment, a %large medical cabinet% and a #small shelf#.
An auto-doc hums quietly in the corner while sterile surfaces and a !medical terminal! reflect the overhead lights.
A door leads back to the *recreation room*. On the opposite wall a hatch leads to the *stasis chamber*.

name: Main Corridor CH-1
description: This passageway connects the main sections of the ship.Overhead lights cast a steady glow on the metal deck plates.
The *recreation room* entrance leads forward towards the bridge. Besides your *captains quarters* is access door to the *crew cabin*.
On the opposite wall is a door labeled 'Restricted Access' *mainframe room*. A *sub corridor CH-2* leads to the cargo hold while the *engineering* section is aft.

name: Mainframe Room
description: The ship's mainframe, responsible for all ship systems, the !mainframe access terminal! can only be accessed by high security clearance personal.
A door leads back to the *main corridor CH-1*.

name: Sub Corridor CH-2
description: A secondary corridor that branches off *main corridor CH-1*.
Less trafficked, with fewer lights and occasional flickering panels.
On the left wall is a door to the *bathroom*
At the end of the corridor are the large *cargo bay* doors

name: Engineering
description: The ship's engineering bay, filled with tools and diagnostic equipment.
The reactor core thrums with contained energy. Close by you see the !engineering terminal!.
A %large parts storage unit% sits next to a long #workbench#, opposite to this is a worn %tool storage cabinet%
A solid door leads back towards *main corridor CH-1*, a large door leads ahead to the *propulsion bay*, while on the left wall a small hatch is labelled *life support*

name: Propulsion Bay
description: The propulsion bay houses the ship's main engines.
The sub light ion drives and FTL jump drive dominate the space with a separate propulsion reactor that provides the massive power required.
Heat radiators glow dull red. The hum of contained energy fills the air. A hatch leads back to *engineering*.

name: Life Support
description: The life support systems room, filled with air and water recycling machinery.
Gauges show oxygen levels and humidity controls. The air is fresh and slightly humid.
A small hatch leads back to *engineering*.

name: Airlock
description: The ship's airlock for spacewalks and docking. EVA suits are racked along one wall.
The outer door seals tightly against the vacuum. An inner hatch leads back to the *cargo bay*.

name: Cargo Bay
description: The main cargo storage area, stacked with containers and crates.
Loading ramps and cargo handlers stand idle. The bay is spacious but dimly lit to save power.
On the left wall next to the *airlock* door is an %EVA equipment locker%. On the opposite wall you see a %large cabinet%, a #shelving unit# and the !cargo manifest terminal!
A large door leads back to *sub corridor CH-2*. At the opposite end of the bay is a hydraulically operated *external door*
